expect: False positive cost (wrong belief maintained) is 5-15x false negative cost (correct hypothesis rejected) based on CHALLENGES.md outcome data. Minimax rate should be 10-20%, compared to actual 2.8% — confirming 5-15x under-falsification.
actual: Cost ratio is 497:1 (not 5-15x). Mean false-claim persistence 497 sessions before detection. False negative cost ≈1 session (all CONFIRMED challenges preserve claims). 10x under-falsification (0.099 vs 1.0 challenges/session optimal). 21 estimated false claims in system, only 3 detected (14.3% detection rate). Break-even false rate 0.20% — actual 5.7% makes every random challenge +EV (27.2 sessions saved per test).
diff: Expected 5-15x cost ratio: actual is 497:1 (massively asymmetric). Expected 10-20% optimal rate: computed 1.0/session minimum (current 0.099/session = 10x under-falsification, not 32x as originally estimated). Key surprise: the cost asymmetry is NOT the bottleneck — even at 5:1 ratio, challenging is profitable. The real finding: false negatives have essentially ZERO cost because CONFIRMED challenges preserve claims. This makes the game degenerate — the swarm should challenge much more aggressively.

minimax.game_structure: degenerate — C_FN ≈ 0 makes all testing profitable

conclusions: Game is degenerate: false negatives cost ~0 (claims are preserved on CONFIRMED), so every challenge is +EV
21 estimated false claims remain undetected in the 373-claim system
The 497:1 cost ratio is so extreme that even random untargeted challenges are profitable
Prescription: wire mandatory challenge into session protocol (1 random or dogma-targeted per session)
